entry.title: Mass Maintenance - Products taking a long time
entry.content:
Hi all,

We are trying to use Mass Maintenance - Products to update some material details (valuation areas in this case) and if the import file contains any more than around 10-20 records the process becomes extremely slow to the point that it stops entirely, even after running for days. There are no errors in the process when we only import a few changes at a time but it is not practical to do this when we need to make around 1700 changes.

Does anyone know what could be causing this problem, or what steps we can take to diagnose what is causing the performance issue?

Thanks

entry.title: MM_EKKO : Unable to archive STO - Error The delivery costs are not cleared for STO
entry.content:
While trying to archive STO type ZUB, getting an error The delivery costs are not cleared for STO in preprocessing step. 

Found the note 334506 - Archiving MM_EKKO: Delivery costs no archiving  relevant for this issue. However, it is not compatible for current system release.

entry.title: Re: "Mastering the Application Rationalization with SAP L...

entry.title: Re: Unable to configure trusted RFC no matter what
entry.content: Dear Symon, please do the following 1. see and perform the solution in KBA 3321072 'Object Directory Entry LIMU COMM does not exist' when implementing TCI 2. see the content in Note 3287611: In case you are on SAP_BASIS relaese 7.31 or 7.40 and downloaded the note 3089413 before 25th February 2023 please apply note 3304520. Please know that Note 3089413 is a bit complicated. Read it carefully. Also share the Kernal version patch and SAP_BASIS version. Best regards, Vera Jiang

entry.title: How to define a selection limit to a Macro Table in SAPUI5 FPM Odata v4
entry.content:
Hi, experts!

I have a freestyle SAPUI5 FPM app consuming an odata v4 service provided by a CAP Project.
I need to set the selection limit for the macro table which the data is listed, because the default value is too low for the customer.

I did some debugging and found the property selectionLimit, but change it did not take any effect to this message above.

At the init method of my controller, I set the selectionLimit like this:
oMacroTable.getTableDefinition().control.selectionLimit = 5000

But when I try to select all records again, the alert message shows as well.

That is the xml code I'm using for the macro table:

Do you know how to properly set the selection limit for a macro table?

Best Regards

entry.title: Step-by-Step Guide: Installing SAP BusinessObjects BI 2025 (aka SAP BI 2025)
entry.content:
Get ready for SAP BI 2025!

Welcome to this comprehensive tutorial on installing SAP BusinessObjects BI 2025, the latest evolution of SAP's trusted business intelligence platform.

As someone who has been working with this technology for over 25 years and writing installation guides since SAP BI 4.1 back in 2013, I know how important a smooth setup process is. This guide will walk you through every step, complete with screenshots and useful links, so you can get SAP BI 2025 up and running without any headaches.

I still can't believe SAP BusinessObjects BI 2025 is here!  Bringing a fresh wave of enhancements that make business intelligence more efficient, secure, and future-ready.  Let’s do this!

Before you start

Here are some useful links I think are worth reading first before going ahead and getting technical!

🚀What's New:

  • Welcome to SAP BusinessObjects BI 2025 by @GregBotticchio
  • Experience Your Favourite BI Platform, BOBJ, in a Fresh New Look with the SAP BI 2025 Release by @VenkateswaraGupthaY
  • SAP BI 2025: What’s New In Web Intelligence and Semantic Layer by @christian_ah-soon 
  • SAP BI 2025: What’s New in Crystal Reports by @donald_guo 
  • Official What's New (SAP Help)

📝Strategy:

  • SAP Analytics Business Intelligence Statement of Direction
  • SAP BI On Premise Maintenance Strategy
  • BI 2025 Maintenance Strategy & Schedule (SAP Note)

General Availability: 12.03.2025 (Version: 14.4.0.5253)
End of Mainstream Maintenance: 31.12.2027

 ⚙️ Technical:

  • 🔥 Supported Platforms (PAM) 
  • ⚠️ Products and features are removed in SAP BusinessObjects BI platform 2025 (SAP Notes)
  • 🆘 SAP Business Intelligence Suite Release Restrictions
  • 💾 SAP BusinessObjects BI 2025 Release Notes
  • User Guides (SAP Help)
  • Installation & Upgrade Guides (SAP Help)
  • Administrator's Guides (SAP Help)

Technical Assessment

Run a Free SAP BI 2025 Readiness Assessment

Wiiisdom offers a free Customer Cockpit in 360Suite that helps assess your organization's readiness for SAP BI 2025. This tool provides valuable statistical insights about your environment, including:

  • The number of UNV universes that will need to be converted to UNX.
  • The number of Crystal Reports for Enterprise that must be converted to Crystal Reports "Classic" or Web Intelligence.
  • The number of Lumira visualizations that will need to be rebuilt in SAP Analytics Cloud or Web Intelligence.
  • The number of Live Office documents that must be rebuilt in SAP Analysis for Microsoft Office or Web Intelligence.
  • The number of documents in Analysis for OLAP that will need to be rebuilt in SAP Analytics Cloud or Web Intelligence.

In addition, the solution provides insights into your Universes' usage, such as:

  • The number of documents still pointing to UNV universes (these will need to be repointed).
  • The number of UNX Multi Source Universes (MSU) that will need to be converted to Single Source Universes (SSU).

Pre-Requisites

License Keys

Before proceeding with a new installation, upgrade, or migration of SAP BusinessObjects BI 2025, it is essential to generate a new License Key. Even if you are upgrading from a previous version, a fresh license key is required to activate BI 2025 successfully.

In addition to reviewing the provided documents and links, make sure to complete this step before installation to avoid any activation issues.

This actually might be the most complicated part of this entire project!

For guidance on generating your SAP BI 2025 license key, refer to the following links:

  • 📌 How to generate license keys for SAP BusinessObjects Business Intelligence Platform (SAP Notes)
  • 📌 SAP provides Emergency License Keys valid for 90-days in case there are issues in obtaining your permanent keys.  Make sure to read the important notes at the top of the PDF.

Software Download

Finding all the necessary installation files for SAP BusinessObjects BI 2025 can sometimes be a bit tricky. If you're having trouble locating them, don't worry—SAP provides a helpful SAP Note that outlines exactly where to download everything you need.

📌 How to download SAP BusinessObjects Business Intelligence (BI) software (SAP Notes)

📌 Shortcut to SAP BusinessObjects BI 2025 Download page

Installation Steps

The steps outlined below are specific to the Windows installation, but the process for Linux is very similar; please refer to the official documentation for more detailed instructions.

The first step is to launch the installer. You can execute the initial file you downloaded, "BIPLATS4400_0-80008823_P1.EXE," or, personally, I prefer using a tool like WinRAR to extract the file to a folder first and then run "setup.exe" to start the installation. In both cases, the second file, "BIPLATS4400_0-80008823_P2.RAR," will be used automatically. Choose whichever method you prefer!

 

 

 

As you can see here, the prerequisite check failed because I needed to address a suppressed reboot. Whatever the issues may be, they will need to be resolved before you can continue.

 

 

 

Although you can change your mind later and select additional languages, I strongly recommend making your decision during the initial installation to avoid lengthy steps and unnecessary risks.

Based on my experience, unless I only require a Web Server, selecting the Web Tier is perfect for the job. In all other cases, I opt for a Full installation and, if necessary, manually disable the Tomcat service. While this may waste some disk space and take a little more time during updates, it ensures that you have a Web Tier available on that application server for troubleshooting purposes.

I would never choose a Custom / Expand installation. In the end, you'll only save a bit of disk space, but you'll reduce your flexibility later on.

Remember: SAP doesn't recommend using the default SQL Anywhere Database for BI in Production.  SAP Note.  Here I'll be using Microsoft SQL Server Express 2022 but below is the screen you'd get if you decided to stick with Sybase SQL Anywhere.

It requires some technical skills but it is possible to change your mind later on and use a different database for the System Database without losing your data.  You'd be doing this in the Central Configuration Manager (CCM).  It's a different story for the Audit Database...

 

 

 

 

Note: In this case, I created my ODBC connections first.  If you forget, you can go ahead at this stage and click Refresh to load them in the installer.

 

 

Note: The SIA Port and the same below for the CMS and Tomcat can be changed easily after the installation.

 

Note: Make sure you keep all these passwords in a vault or something.  The CMS Cluster Key is needed for a number of things like clustering servers for instance.

 

 

 

 

 

 🥳 And... Done

About screen from the Central Management Console (CMC):

 About screen from the BI launch pad:

Post-Installation Configuration

When logging into the Central Management Console (CMC) for the first time, you will be prompted with the System Configuration Wizard. While this can be done at a later stage and is available from the main menu, I recommend completing it sooner rather than later.

In short, this wizard will guide you through the process of splitting and configuring your Adaptive Processing Servers (APS). The more RAM you have, the more options you'll be able to configure.

This isn't a new wizard, but I’m always surprised to see production environments where the APS aren’t optimally configured. This is a great place to start, and be sure to consult the administrator's and sizing guides for more detailed information.

 

 

 

Note: As you can see, selecting 15-25 GB RAM will create 7 APS on this node.  The default installation only has 1 APS.

 

 

 

 

 

Conclusion

I'm sure you're as excited as I am about this new release, and I hope this guide has been helpful to you.

There’s a lot of great documentation out there, but it can be all over the place and difficult to find. My goal with these guides remains the same: to provide what I think are the most useful and important resources all in one place.

Feel free to share your comments and suggestions!

Thanks, and take care.

entry.title: Page Footer Supress Does Not Work Properly
entry.content:
This is a problem I've had since 2011 at the beginning of Crystal Reports, and it still occurs today in the 2020 SP4/P4 version (14 year of problem).

When a page footer is defined and suppressed without formula, the detail takes advantage of the entire sheet with the records.

When it is suppressed via formula, it does not take over the space.

SAP Crystal Reports 

See code Archive RPT example 

Rgs

Fabio

SAP 10 FP2502 / SQL SERVER 2019 / SAP HANA

entry.title: Re: Step-by-Step Guide: Installing SAP BusinessObjects BI 2025 (aka SAP BI 2025)
entry.content:
Happy to see the installation sequence flown smoothly.

#BI2025 #SAP #BOBJ

entry.title: I registered for the CER006 but had medical issues didn’t use any. Anyone had extension
entry.content: I bought the CER006 but was very sick last year . Just getting on my feet now . Anyone ever had an extension ? Or will they give me any credit . Because I didn’t use any attempt??? @SAPLearningHub

entry.title: 资产会计 - SAP S4HANA Cloud（公共版）2502 版本中的资产重估新功能介绍
entry.content:
背景介绍

我们会计记录的都是历史成本，但是随着社会经济的发展，会计上记录的过去的金额不能反映真实公允的价值了，因此准则允许企业进行重估。例如2010年工厂购买的商业地产，记录在账面上的价值是30万元，但是2020的时候价值就变成了60万，那么现在账面上记录的30万元就不能真实公允的体现出实际的价值，所以需要重估。

对于手动资产重估，可使用“过账资产重估”应用程序 (F7775)。之前，您可以同时以所有或多个货币重估资产。通常，以多种货币执行重估。

但是，在某些情况下，您只需要以本币执行重估，而不会影响集团货币（硬通货币）。

新业务交易类型“以所选货币重估”可用于此目的。使用此新业务交易类型，可以单一（所选）货币过账重估并报告。

这意味着您现在可以区分标准重估情况（所有受影响的货币）和特定重估（所有分类账中只有一种货币）。

测试场景

资产于 2024 年 12 月 31 日购置，折旧码为 CD01。使用年限为 4 年。折旧方法为直线折旧。残值率百分比为 5%

购置和生产成本交易(APC):

100,000

2025年计划普通折旧:

-23,750

2025年底净账面值:

76,250

资产购置之后

2025年的计划折旧金额的计算公式是100000*(1-5%)/48*12=23750

由于通货膨胀，需要在 2024 年末进行重估：资产的账面价值将重估 20%。

在过账资产重估应用的选择屏幕上，指定以下值：

例如：

  • 20,000 APC 的重估
  • 5000 的普通折旧的重估

对于手动重估，可以使用过账资产重估应用 (F7775)。之前，您可以使用该应用同时以所有或多个货币重估。通常以多种货币执行重估。

但是，在某些情况下，您需要仅以本币执行重估，而集团货币（硬通货币）不受影响。

2502版本支持对于特定重估（所有分类账中仅一种货币），在字段业务交易类型选择“重估(所选货币)”

资产购置和重估之后

经过重估之后，2025年的计划折旧金额的计算公式是[(100000+20000）*(1-5%)-5000)/48*12=27250

除手动重估外，自 2502 版本起，新应用维护资产重估指数引入了有关在特定折旧范围中使用重估指数自动重估的功能。

如果要定期执行基于指数值的自动重估，则使用此应用。

可在链接中找到详细说明

  1. 创建重估索引（强制）

2.将索引标识分配到资产主记录（必填）

3.检查固定资产评估视图中的价值（可选）

4.检查资产历史数据表中的值（可选）

此处共享步骤的一些结果:

步骤 1：创建重估索引（必填）

步骤 2：将索引标识分配到资产主记录（必填）

步骤 3：检查管理固定资产应用的评估视图中的值（可选）

根据分配给资产的重估索引，重估金额和折旧金额将按如下设计进行计划。

对于2024年

对于2025年

对于2026年

对于2027年

步骤 4：检查资产历史数据表中的值（可选）

总结

这一新功能使得管理资产重估更加灵活，使您能够更有效地处理特定重估场景。

entry.title: Integration Insights Live: Innovate with a modern iPaaS
entry.content:
Leading businesses find the core of generating tangible outcomes from investments in enterprise AI is an integrated business landscape using integration as a service (iPaaS). Integrations make scaling AI across enterprises possible because it allows democratic information sharing across users regardless of the systems and apps used in the enterprise and no matter where the teams are located.  

Today, it is well understood that only by connecting the multitude of solutions and facilitating seamless information exchange can you truly unlock intelligence, simplify innovation, and lower your total cost of ownership. The approach of building point-to-point integrations to connect individual software systems is long gone. It lacked scale and introduced a whole new layer of complexity where every new system being added to the landscape required custom code or connectors to communicate with existing systems. Integration as a service allows you to extract maximum value from the enterprise platforms that have been invested and drive real results.

With SAP Integration Suite on SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) you can easily address your integration needs using modern enterprise integration patterns such as API-based and Event-driven integrations. To learn more about its extensive functionality, join us at our upcoming virtual summit - Integration insights live: Innovate with a modern iPaaS. We’ve lined up five sessions exploring the key features, sharing real-world use cases, discussing value benefits, as well as providing an overview of the latest innovations and exciting road map.

Keynote

AI, modernization, and automation initiatives are top of mind for many IT leaders today as they grapple with increasing application landscape diversity and resource constraints. So what technologies are used to drive success of these projects? Hear Shari Lava, senior director, AI and automation  at IDC and Achim Kraiss, chief product officer for SAP Business Technology Platform discuss the strategic value of an iPaaS.

CONA Services: Accelerating innovation with SAP Integration Suite

This inspiring session features our customer CONA Services LLC, an IT services company for the 12 largest independently owned Coca-Cola bottling companies in North America. You’ll hear firsthand how the organization boosted bottlers’ business growth and competitive advantage by integrating multiple solutions and third-party applications on SAP Integration Suite.

Unleash the power of AI-driven integration for complex application landscapes

For a holistic view of integration scenarios with scalable, real-time integration of business processes, APIs, and events across SAP and third-party landscapes. I will be the speaker of this session. Do join in to learn about the comprehensive capabilities offered by SAP Integration Suite.

Connect all your applications with prebuilt integrations and adapters

If you’re struggling with a growing landscape of diverse applications and it’s making connected business processes a challenge, this session is for you! SAP Integration Suite offers hundreds of prebuilt adapters and connectors to many other applications such as those from Salesforce, ServiceNow, NetSuite, Snowflake, and more. Allowing you to connect your applications quickly, with these adapters you can avoid using multiple integration solutions for different applications.

Solving connectivity chaos to improve AI outcomes

These days, modern integration tools don’t just solve today’s complex connectivity challenges, they also enable improved AI outcomes. IDC’s Shari Lava returns to the stage to share her research findings on how modern integration platforms unlock business value from AI investments.

Don’t miss this opportunity to learn more about SAP Integration Suite and how it can support your integration needs. Whether you are looking to elevate operational efficiency or deliver digital transformation and improve AI outcomes, simplified integration is your key to success.

Register now to secure your spot. We look forward to seeing you there!

entry.title: How to get a report for Plan Revenue, Estimated Costs, manual POC
entry.content:
Dear SAP Team,
We have to enter under specific circumstances the Plan Revenue, Estimated Costs or manual POC in Sales Orders.

For Audit and controlling reasons we need an overview for which SO we have entered such data. I made a research in the system and in the "characteristic index" and did not find those field. Could you please help us to get detail information for those fields?

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to get a report for Plan Revenue, Estimated Costs...
entry.content:
Dear customer, 

You can check the following reports for further details: (Project Cost and Revenue Details by Project, Project Cost and Revenue Details by Project Structure, Project Cost and Revenue by Project, Project Cost and Revenue by Project Structure and Project Plan Values). 

For more details please refer the Help documentation and KBA below:

Project Cost and Revenue Details by Project
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BUSINESS_BYDESIGN/2754875d2d2a403f95e58a41a9c7d6de/2c26e905722d10148afea37d9417851c.html?locale=en-US

2559087 - How-To and Consulting Queries in Reporting for Analytics https://me.sap.com/notes/2559087

entry.title: How to defining credit master data in the business partner
entry.content:
Dear SAP

 

How to defining credit master data in the business partner?

Thanks and regards

 

PERSON_NAME


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to defining credit master data in the business pa...
entry.content:
It’s a guidance on Defining Credit Master Data in the Business Partner.

  1. Go to “Manage Your Solution”, select the Application Area as “Finance” and the Sub Application Area as “Credit Evaluation and Management”, in the Master Data, please make the following configuration:
    a. Create Credit Segments
    b. Create Risk Classes
    c. Create Rule for Scoring and Credit Limit Calculation


    In Process and Business Transactions, make the following configuration:
    a. Define Checking Rules


  2. Navigate to app Schedule Credit Management Jobs, and create with the job template Assign Credit Management Role to Business Partners.

entry.title: Re: COMP - To obtain Compensation Statement online
entry.content: Hi Xavier, Thanks for your replies! 'Online' here means that these groups can receive their Comp Statement automatically, without HR having to generate and send it manually. It's more like an automated process where, once we publish it, they might receive it via email.

entry.title: #CoE#LAL How to set delivery block for a sales order with customer down payment
entry.content: In best practice, after creating a sales order with customer down payment, it should has a block for delivery and it will be removed utill prepayment is received. When this block is missing, how can I set this block in implementation activity?

entry.title: Re: #CoE#LAL Cannot create sales order with customer down...
entry.content:
Hi Patryk, if free goods item category is TANN( it's the line item that generated automatically when the count of goods reach the restriction of discount type YA00), it does have price but 100% discount. Whether it can see as a kind of sales?

Such as CBXN, this kind of item has price and 100% discount, so this item has billing. And CBLN, it's also free goods, but it doesn't has price , so no billing for that.

If TANN can be seen as a kind of sales, so it should has billing?

entry.title: Heatmap-Driven Testing for SAP S/4HANA: A Systematic Approach Using UiPath Test Suite
entry.content:
Software testing in SAP S/4HANA projects often requires extensive collaboration between functional teams, business analysts, and quality assurance professionals to validate business-critical processes comprehensively. However, given the large number of transactions in an SAP system, manually prioritizing test cases can lead to inefficiencies, delays, and gaps in coverage.

This article explores how UiPath Test Suite’s SAP Heatmap can be leveraged as a systematic method for test case generation by identifying high-risk transactions based on key system attributes.

A heatmap-based approach to test design is rooted in risk-based testing (RBT) principles, where test identification and prioritization are determined by quantifiable risk indicators. The UiPath SAP Heatmap, an integrated feature of Test Manager, provides a visual representation of transactional importance based on the following factors:

  • User Engagement (#Users) – Number of users interacting with a transaction (T-Code, Fiori app, or report).
  • Execution Frequency (#Executions) – How often a transaction is executed within a defined period.
  • Processing Time (Performance) – The cumulative system processing time for each transaction.
  • Complexity (#Objects) – The number of SAP development objects involved (e.g., screens, functional modules, tables).
  • Transaction Type (Intensity) – Classification of transactions as Create, Change, or Display operations.

The heatmap thereby serves as an empirical model for test case selection, ensuring that testing efforts are focused on transactions with the highest business and technical significance.

By leveraging heatmap insights, QA teams can focus their testing where it matters most - transactions with the highest business impact and technical complexity.

After generating the SAP Heatmap, teams can filter by module (e.g., Finance, Logistics, Procurement) to identify areas that require testing. For this example, we focus on the Logistics module.

The heatmap visually scales transaction importance—the larger the transaction in the heatmap, the more frequently it is used. Let’s choose MMBE (Stock Overview) - a heavily utilized transaction in inventory management.

With a single click on "Generate Tests", UiPath Test Suite automatically creates structured test cases. To enhance test accuracy and traceability, testers can integrate supporting documentation and prompts into the test case repository, including:

✔ BPMN Diagrams – Business process flow models.
✔ SAP Functional Documentation – Blueprints outlining process expectations.
✔ Test Data Definitions – Pre-configured input datasets.
✔ Prompts – Define testing goals such as system/ integration/ regression

These artifacts provide a comprehensive reference for both manual and automated test execution.

You will now have a list of detailed, structured test cases generated, including:

✔ Test Steps – A step-by-step breakdown of the transaction flow.
✔ Expected Results – Pass/fail conditions per step.
✔ Test Data – Reusable input values.
✔ Automation Potential – Readiness for automated execution.

 

This article focused on a data-driven approach enabled by UiPath Test Suite, to streamline test case generation based on system usage patterns, transaction frequency, and business criticality. By systematically identifying high-risk transactions, QA teams can:

✔ Optimize test resource allocation.
✔ Reduce overall testing effort and costs.
✔ Enhance test automation readiness.

entry.title: From Chatbot Assistants to Strategic Copilots and Agents:Advancing Your Procurement Strategy with AI
entry.content: Join Pierre Mitchell (Chief Research Officer and Managing Director, Spend Matters) and Barri Horn (Director of Product Marketing, SAP Finance and Spend Management) to explore the transformative impact of AI on procurement and how chatbots, strategic copilots, and agents are revolutionizing operations. ​

entry.title: Re: Dynamic Text in manually added columns
entry.content:
appreciating your response @nikhil Here is a screenshot of the calculated column "Business Plan 2025."

The column is calculated by summing the Outlook and Business Plan 2025 values from the BW Query, which is currently hidden. This column has been derived by applying a simple summation formula. The heading for this column is part of a dimension labeled as "Category."

However, the category columns are the original ones that have been replaced with manually created columns. If the dimension is used to generate dynamic text, the original column reappears. Therefore, the goal is to make it dynamic based on the fiscal year dimension.

entry.title: Re: FD32- Current Credit Limit and Maximum permitted credit limits
entry.content:
For example,

If customer has Credit limit assigned (CCA1) 1000

Maximum permitted Individual limit 3000

Sale order 2000

for this case, is the system blocked?

entry.title: Re: SAP RAP Static Action( Function Import ) not working
entry.content:
Hi @Bhavik2,

You've to pass the action parameters at URI level and not at the payload something as mentioned below - 

/sap/opu/odata/sap/SERVICE_V2/testStaticAction?Param1='10'&Param2='20'

Hope this resolves your query.

Please accept the solution if it does.

Regards,

ClearQueries.com 

Follow on LinkedIn

entry.title: Re: 关于中国 balance sheet 和 PL报表的配置问题请教
entry.content:

entry.title: Re: Inter Company Individual Purchase order!
entry.content:
Hi,

I have also implemented the same scenario with IPO Process. 

Plant A - Company code A, Plant B - Company code A, Customer 

Flow

> SO - From Plant B to Customer (TAB Item category with E Special Stock indicator and schedule item CB with Account assignment M and Stock transfer)

> Creates PR (With Requirement Class KEB with special stock E)  

> PR converted to STO (From Plant A to Plant B - Intra company transfer) 

> OBD created for STO with Movement type 641 

> While PGI getting the error " Sales order stock does not exist " M7 184 

The Sales order stock being check during STO - PGI, Can anyone please let me know if you solved this issue? 

Thanks

Sunil

entry.title: Re: Usage of Quantity Based- Inspection Point in Public C...
entry.content:
@EndaDear Ma'am, we have created an inspection point based on quantity in Define Inspection Point (1022430) and assigned it to the routing, but it is not functioning as expected. Upon investigation, we found that in the GUI, under the Routing (Tcode - CA02) in Inspection Points tab, there is an option for a quantity-related radio button where we can enter the Quantity Interval and its UOM. However, this field/radio button is not available in the Public Cloud.(Please find attached screenshots of both fields). In my opinion, this is the reason why quantity-based inspection point is not working in Public Cloud.

 

 

Please Help,

Thanks.

Kind Regards,

Ajit

entry.title: Re: Functional Currency differs from Company Code Currency
entry.content:
Hi Derek,

Before release, viewing the standard cost calculation in the functional currency is not possible.
For the material cost estimate, we support Controlling Area Currency and Object/Company Code Currency, which applies to all deployment options.
Upon release, the cost is translated into all parallel currencies.

Material cost estimates are calculated and stored in the Controlling Area Currency and Object Currency, then translated into the additional currencies defined for the ledger at the time of release.

Regards,

Venu

entry.title: Re: SAC Input control Issue
entry.content: I would recommend to create a ticket to SAP on this.

entry.title: Error - profit center or business area is different in the contract and the valuation object
entry.content:
Hi all,

After changing the cost centre and profit centre for a lease contract under Contract Management > Organizational Assignment Rule > Profit Center, we are getting the following error when trying to process the valuation:

"The profit center or business area is different in the contract and the valuation object"

The profit centre for the contract in the error message is still showing the old value. Unsure where this is coming from. Is anyone able to please advise where to go to change this?

Thanks

entry.title: Re: How to Extract Long Text from ECC and Prepare a DMC Template for Loading Long Text into S4/HANA
entry.content:
Hi Community,

"When working with SAP Business Objects Data Services (BODS), a common challenge is combining multiple rows into a single row. While BODS provides a built-in reverse pivot transform for this purpose, it requires a predefined number of rows to concatenate. This constraint becomes a hurdle when the row count is unknown or variable."

 

Thanks for your selfless sharing, it really help us a lot.

🙂

 

Regards,

ZFiori.

entry.title: Re: Get Data from https://smard.api.bund.dev/ not working (complete ABAP-Code is in post)
entry.content:
Hi M-K,

thx for the reply. But this did not solve the issue 😞

thanks regards,

Mario

entry.title: Beyond SAP Cloud - Episode 10: BTP Cloud Foundry Spaces
entry.content:
In this episode, we explore the concept of spaces within SAP's Business Technology Platform (BTP). Spaces allow for organized development environments within sub-accounts, enabling selective access to applications, such as HR or finance, ensuring sensitive data is secured. We differentiate spaces from subaccounts, focusing on the benefits of spaces for project management and access control. Additionally, we highlight the importance of memory quota management for spaces, with a look ahead to further exploration of quota configurations in the next episode. Spaces offer a streamlined way to manage resources and permissions efficiently.

 

Takeaways

  • Spaces in BTP allow additional organizational levels for Cloud Foundry runtime in subaccounts.
  • Cloud Foundry runtime offers the ability to create spaces for specific project deployments.
  • Developers can be given restricted access to specific spaces, ensuring security for sensitive applications.
  •  The main difference between a space and a subaccount is that business users are associated with subaccounts, not spaces.
  • In scenarios where identifying different business users is needed, a new subaccount is required.
  • If business user identification is not necessary, adding an additional space is recommended.
  • The same Cloud connector can be used in multiple spaces, benefiting from the same configuration in the sub-account.
  • Roles and trust configurations are managed at the subaccount level, not the space level.
  • Spaces require configuration of quotas, including memory allocation for Cloud Foundry.
  • Quotas in spaces provide full control over the memory consumed by Cloud Foundry in each space.
  • The next episode will dive deeper into how to assign quotas for better resource management.

entry.content:
Hi @Glyba_Dmitrii ,

Could you please check below in WT/WO document, pass the correct resource in your SAP user and let us know any questions.

Reason for error: your assigned resource with your SAP (API)USER ID is not the same the assigned Resource of WO. 

1. Check the Warehouse Order document of Warehouse Task, is there any resource assigned ?

2. Assign the above resource into your Your SAP API USER. 

Kind Regards,

Ajit

entry.title: Deletable default user
entry.content:
Hi Community,.

We are using Private Cloud Edition of S/4HANA.
We are implementing S/4HANA 2023.
There are several S/4 users that customers (including our implementation partners) have not registered.
Is it safe to delete these? Or is it ok to lock the users?

_SAP_FIORI... This is the user that SAP uses when initially building the environment, and I believe it can be deleted.
_SAP_TEC_USR... This is the user who accesses the HANA DB behind the scenes and I am aware of it. Is it safe to lock it?
BGRFC_SUPER... This is the recognition used for the RFC connection. Is it safe to lock it?

entry.title: Disable Automated Update
entry.content:
Hello,


I have already searched SAP forums, but I have not found any information regarding disabling "Automated Update" in Manage Remote Connections so that it does not automatically check for updates every 7 days. This is interfering when SAP changes the environment, because the Database Server IP Address changes and then the connection stops working. I would like to know how to disable the Recent Activity and Automated Update fields and what the impact is on the environment.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Disable Automated Update
entry.content: Note: for on-premise systems, if Automated Update is set to "Yes", it indicates system data is updated from SAP Solution Manager (not in the System Data).

entry.title: Re: SAP HANA calculation view version rollback
entry.content: nice blog. Thank you.

entry.title: EOIO in SAP CI Using JMS - Exclusive Mode
entry.content:
Introduction


EOIO was a long awaited functionality that every Integration consultant was waiting for in SAP CI. We had this in SAP PO where we would use it to preserve the order of messages. Messages using EOIO were stored in a FIFO queue that ensured the messages that arrive first in the middleware is also the first to leave. The order was ensured even in case of outages on the receiver system and the middleware would continue to retry the failed messages in the context of FIFO.

Solution


SAP has recently released JMS Adapter Adapter version 1.4, that has the Exclusive mode which ensures that the message order is preserved.


Both JMS Sender and Receiver adapters have a new option now - Access type - Exclusive / Non-Exclusive 

For normal scenarios where the Order in which the messages are processed doesn't matter it's highly recommended to use non-exclusive setting in Access Type. Using "Exclusive" as Access type will enforce EOIO and takes away the option of parallel processing which ultimately means that as soon as the receiver system is up messages will get processed one by one and not parallelly. 

Let's dive in to the POC now.

Here we have a integration process that has this message which is split into 5 individual messages and will be sent to a EOIO JMS Queue 

 

Content Modifier has the following message

<Root>
  <Message>
    <id>1</id>
  </Message>
  <Message>
    <id>2</id>
  </Message>
  <Message>
    <id>3</id>
  </Message>
  <Message>
    <id>4</id>
  </Message>
  <Message>
    <id>5</id>
  </Message>
</Root>

 We are splitting this and sending individual Message nodes to the JMS Queue, Parallel processing is unchecked in the splitter

  

 The Integration process runs and creates 5 messages in the JMS Queue 

Now SAP CI iflow will automatically retry these as the receiver systems URL is a dummy URL which doesn't exist

As expected only the very first message gets Retried and none of the other messages in the queue are picked up.

 

 This is a very welcome update from SAP. This will bridge the gap between SAP PO and SAP CI where consultants had to design workarounds to implement EOIO.

entry.title: I want to plan the production date of the finished product based on the delivery date of the parts.
entry.content:
Dear SAP Experts

Hello.

When the delivery date of a component is pushed back, we want to revise the production plan for semi-finished products produced using the component and finished products produced using the semi-finished products in a batch.

I know that this is possible by using Heuristic Rescheduling: Bottom-Up for Conti- I/O, but the plan is not revised until the last finished product.

Is heuristic customization not a good idea?
Should we use a different heuristic?
I would like your advice.

I report in the attachment the situation that is not working.

Best regards

Nagase

I believe the options with "SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity" and the "Bank Statement - Post API" might be of interest to you. 

Best regards,

Anita

entry.title: Re: Messages in HOLDING status SAP PO
entry.content:
Go to http(s)://server:port/pimon

From there you see this submenu:

In the right end of the list you see:

so in my case you have to repeat now 3 times the activity. Click on the GUID hyperlinked on "Blocked Message ID". So what ever makes sense (either restart of cancel). Then you refresh the display, and you repeat until the line disappeared from the refresh.

entry.title: Re: 🇦🇪 SAP Build: Create Business Apps and Processes Without Code (Dubai, UAE)
entry.content: Hi @DayanaMathew - The confirmation emails were already sent to those who registered before Mar 18th, you should have received the confirmation email now.

entry.title: Re: onInit hook is not executing from controller
entry.content:
There might be multiple ways to achieve this, but what works for me has a slight difference to your code: I use an implementation using "overrides" from the "ControllerExtension" class (instead of "Controller" class):

sap.ui.define([
    'sap/ui/core/mvc/ControllerExtension',
    'sap/ui/model/Sorter'
], function (ControllerExtension, Sorter) {
    'use strict'

  return ControllerExtension.extend("managetags.ext.controller.ObjectPageExt", {
    override: {
      onInit: function () { ... }
    }
  })
})

See also: https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/sdk/#/topic/21515f09c0324218bb705b27407f5d61.html

2. I have set up the rejection quantity. How can I enable automatic decision-making? How can I configure the system to automatically determine the decision based on the table I created, eliminating the manual decision step?

entry.title: Dynamic Date Range Selector in SAC Story based on DSP Live Model
entry.content:
Hi experts and fellow learner,

I want to create a dynamic Actual/Plan report (not planning enabled). The desired output looks like forecast layout (but doesn't have to be a forecast layout as long as the period ranges of each version can be dynamically displayed) as below.

For example:

Within a year, Jan - July is displayed in Actual Version, Aug - Dec is in Plan Version.

 

As a side note, I'll consume the data from Datasphere Live Model (Analytic model - Live) instead of Import/Standalone Model.

Here's my concern which leads to the question whether this is achievable or not:

When I try to consume the data of DSP analytic (Live) model in SAC story, I don't see any option to filter member by range in the builder pane (as opposed to import model). As far as I understand, this is required in order to select the "current date" - which acts as "Actual Cut-Over Date" and can be selected on the fly. Which leads to question, is there any limitation data consumption in story of DSP Live Data Model in regards to this?


Is there any workable solution to achieve the desired output? Any input would be appreciated. Thank you.

entry.title: getting content type error when make remote service call
entry.content:
hello,

looking at an example from SAP blogs link below, followed all the steps but started getting error when reached at the step https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/cap-extend-sfsf-add-logic.html not sure but when trying SFSF_user service keep getting error 'error during remote service call Received content-type 'text/html' which is not part of accepted content types" all other services are working fine so connectivity is not an issue

any help will be appreciated.

 

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Problem with Submit program( S_ALR_87013611 )
entry.content:
Hi Naresh, 

 Please add below given piece of code:

wa_selection-selname = 'OUT_APPD'.
  wa_selection-kind    = 'P'.  "S-Select-options P-Parameters
  wa_selection-sign    = 'I'.
  wa_selection-option  = 'EQ'.
  wa_selection-low     = abap_true.
  APPEND wa_selection TO it_selection.
  CLEAR: wa_selection.

Also please check this link: https://me.sap.com/notes/0002974084

Hopefully you will get all the cost center details. It worked for me.

Thanks,

entry.title: Re: Outbound communication from SAP Public cloud to a Web...

I will try the HTTP approach now even if that means i must abandon the Jco and implement a HTTP server. The Business connector is also a alternative that i will check in the next days. Thanks for the reply.

entry.title: Re: Parallel crcy area (crcy type 30) missing for area 01...
entry.content: Problem is solved. Reason of the error was a missing accounting standard (IFRS) in parallel accounting:.

Rimjhim Agrawal

entry.title: Re: Condition contract settlement using incorrect VAT cod...
entry.content:
The case was solved with the following change:

In the customizing step: SPRO - Logistics – general - Settlement management - Settlement documents - Settlement document types - All document types make sure that the field "Tax determination method" is set to "Transfer from pricing" on the settlement document type you use.

entry.title: Maintenance Scheduling process
entry.content:
Hi Team, 

Can anyone help me in sharing SAP helpline portal of best practices information about Maintenance Scheduling process in plant maintenance. Which could cover steps of process and business process diagram to show the process and prepare a business process document 

Thanks

]

entry.title: Re: 物料異動類型種類區分的影響與處理
entry.content: Good job

entry.title: ABAP Warning The expression that contains "BUKRS" can get null values since it contains fields...
entry.content:
Hello,

I've got this ABAP warning for all key fields of ZTABLE (BUKRS, etc.) of below ABAP code:

The expression that contains "BUKRS" can get null values since it contains fields that were not created with "Initial Values" in the dictionary.

ABAP code (there's a list of columns and a WHERE clause with IN and = followed by ABAP variables, no SQL expression):

INSERT ztable FROM ( SELECT bukrs, ... FROM zcview ).

BUKRS is the first key field of ZTABLE. ZTABLE has a client key field in the very first position.

ZCVIEW is a CDS entity view with this code (only inner join, no left outer join):

define view entity ZCVIEW
  as select from bkpf
  inner join ...
{
  key bkpf.bukrs,
  ...
  sum( ... ) as sum_...,
  ...
  cast(' ' as belnr_d) as extbelnr,
  ...
}
where ... // conditions against constants
group by ... // all fields before the sum fields

The description says to use ##NULL_VALUES to remove the warning. This works fine.

I'd like to know the reason why there is this warning.

I see in the ABAP documentation INSERT dbtab, source - Alternative 3 ( SELECT subquery_clauses ... ):

Null values to be inserted can be produced in the following cases:

  • As results of outer joins in the FORM clause of the subquery.
  • As results of SQL expressions in the SELECT list of the subquery.
  • If a field read by the subquery already contains a null value.

A catchable exception of exception class CX_SY_OPEN_SQL_DB is raised in the case of columns that are key fields of the DDIC database table or DDIC view to be filled. If it is known statically that null values can be inserted into key fields, a syntax check warning that can be hidden by the pragma null_values is produced.

There is no reason that NULL values can be inserted in the column BUKRS of ZTABLE because the source is the key column BUKRS of BKPF.

Is there a way to avoid the warning without using ##NULL_VALUES?

In what situation is the warning not sent with INSERT ztable FROM ( SELECT ... )?

Thank you.

Sandra

ABAP release: 7.58.

entry.title: Re: SLT: Add field to target structure from logging table
entry.content:
I have now managed it. There is an old and a new loading mechanism.
You can switch to the old replication mechanism using report IUUC_REPL_SWITCH_REC_MCHNSM from note 3448296. The _it_io_s_logtab is then usually also available.
However, you should note that the old replication mechanism has some limitations, e.g. no CDS views can be used with it.
https://help.sap.com/doc/9d634180094347038e7b8c96661dc36b/latest/en-US/Compatibility_Views_and_SAP_LT_Replication_Server.pdf 
It would of course be great from SAP if the _it_io_s_logtab table was also filled with the new replication mechanism.

Translated with DeepL.com (free version)

entry.title: Re: Issue with Query Returning Incorrect Data from MARM T...
entry.content:
You are right. I have adjusted the code, but I am still not getting the desired result. The result remains the same.

DATA: lv_umren_ea TYPE P,
      lv_matnr TYPE MARM-MATNR.
CLEAR lv_umren_ea.
CLEAR lv_matnr.
SELECT SINGLE MATNR
INTO lv_matnr
FROM MARM
WHERE MARM~MEINH = 'EA'.
SELECT SINGLE MARM~UMREN
INTO lv_umren_ea
FROM MARM
JOIN MARC ON MARM~MATNR = MARC~MATNR
JOIN CAUFV ON MARC~WERKS = CAUFV~WERKS
WHERE MARM~MEINH = 'EA'
  AND MARM~MATNR = lv_matnr.
Z_EA = lv_umren_ea.

entry.title: Re: I registered for the CER006 but had medical issues didn’t use any. Anyone had extension
entry.content: When did you register for the Certification specifically? I think when you buy your Certification Exam you have a year to take the attempt. Did this period Ended?

entry.title: SAP Customer Data Cloud Audit Log for Application
entry.content:
Dear All , 

I am looking for all the audit logs for Application User ( API user) , is it the same REST API audit.search to be used to extract the logs using the parameter - userKey ?

Many Thanks,

Ruchita

entry.title: Re: Hands-on Tutorial: Creating a custom AI agent on the BTP
entry.content:
Great blog, thanks for sharing.
Looking at the coding, it looks like you did not provide any description for the tools and no instruction on how to use them (input and output parameters) except via code comments. Does that mean the langgraph library is providing the code comments to the AI Agent via system message?
